solution: 
```java
class TextEditor {
    private StringBuilder text;
    private int cursor;

    public TextEditor() {
        text = new StringBuilder();
        cursor = 0;
    }
    
    public void addText(String textToAdd) {
        text.insert(cursor, textToAdd);
        cursor += textToAdd.length();
    }
    
    public int deleteText(int k) {
        int charsToDelete = Math.min(k, cursor);
        text.delete(cursor - charsToDelete, cursor);
        cursor -= charsToDelete;
        return charsToDelete;
    }
    
    public String cursorLeft(int k) {
        int move = Math.min(k, cursor);
        cursor -= move;
        return text.substring(Math.max(0, cursor - 10), cursor);
    }
    
    public String cursorRight(int k) {
        int move = Math.min(k, text.length() - cursor);
        cursor += move;
        return text.substring(Math.max(0, cursor - 10), cursor);
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a `StringBuilder` to store the text and tracks the cursor position. For `addText`, it inserts the text at the cursor position and updates the cursor. For `deleteText`, it deletes characters to the left of the cursor. For `cursorLeft` and `cursorRight`, it moves the cursor within bounds and returns the last 10 characters to the left. The time complexity is O(N) due to the operations on `StringBuilder`, and the space complexity is O(N) for storing the text.
